answer:
Here's Hono's `.fetch` signature, from `src/hono-base.ts:473-479`:

```ts
fetch: (
  request: Request,
  Env?: E['Bindings'] | {},
  executionCtx?: ExecutionContext
) => Response | Promise<Response>
```

### What you need to know for mounting

**It's a property, not a prototype method.** It's defined as an arrow-function class field, so `this` is bound — you can pass `app.fetch` directly without needing `.bind(app)`:

```ts
// Both work:
otherFramework.mount('/api', app.fetch)
otherFramework.mount('/api', (req) => app.fetch(req))
```

**The signature is intentional and architectural.** Per ADR-01 (a *hard* constraint in the Hono atlas):

> "Runtime-specific integration must live in `src/adapter/<runtime>/**`, translating native event/request shapes into a Web Standard `Request`, calling `app.fetch(request, env, ctx)`, and shaping the Response back."
>
> *Rationale:* "Isolates runtime-specific code from core so the same `app.fetch` signature works everywhere."

So the three-arg `(request, env, ctx)` shape is the universal entry point — every official adapter (Cloudflare Workers/Pages, Bun, Deno, Lambda, service-worker, etc.) ultimately produces this exact call. Mimic that and you're aligned with how Hono itself integrates with hosts.

### Mounting tips

- **`request`** must be a Web Standard `Request`. If your outer framework uses Node `IncomingMessage` (Express/Fastify/etc.), convert it first — or use `@hono/node-server`'s adapters which already do the translation.
- **`Env`** is optional and runtime-specific (Cloudflare bindings, etc.). Pass `{}` or omit if you don't have one.
- **`executionCtx`** is optional — supplies `waitUntil`/`passThroughOnException`. Omit for most non-Workers hosts.
- The return type is `Response | Promise<Response>` (sync or async) — always handle it as a promise on the caller side to be safe.

### Sibling method worth knowing

If you're testing or invoking from code that has a URL/path string rather than a `Request`, there's `app.request(input, requestInit?, Env?, executionCtx?)` right next to it (line 493) — it constructs the `Request` for you and delegates to `.fetch`.

    RATIONALE "Mechanical enforcement so reviewers don't need judgment to catch violations."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Core code must be built entirely on Web Standard APIs (Request, Response, Headers, URL, fetch, ReadableStream, TextEncoder/Decoder, Web Crypto, AbortController, Blob, FormData) with no runtime-specific globals or imports."
    RATIONALE "Runtime-specific imports in core cause the framework to fail to load on Cloudflare Workers and silently pull polyfills on Deno; only the WHATWG Fetch standard is common across all nine supported runtimes."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Middleware in `src/middleware/**` must prefer Web Standards; runtime-specific imports are only allowed if cross-runtime in practice."
    RATIONALE "Maintains portability of middleware across all supported runtimes."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Node-specific adapter code must stay outside core in the separate `@hono/node-server` package, not in `src/adapter/`."
    RATIONALE "Adding it to src/adapter/ would pull Node types into the main hono package and affect every non-Node user's type resolution."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Runtime-specific integration must live in `src/adapter/<runtime>/**`, translating native event/request shapes into a Web Standard Request, calling app.fetch(request, env, ctx), and shaping the Response back."
    RATIONALE "Isolates runtime-specific code from core so the same app.fetch signature works everywhere; the adapter's job is to produce that call, not modify app behavior."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "The `hono` package must have zero runtime dependencies; only devDependencies are permitted."
    RATIONALE "Runtime deps may not exist on all targeted runtimes, bloat bundles, and expand the supply chain; users install `hono` expecting portability and zero bundle cost."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "The only sanctioned `node:` import in middleware is `node:async_hooks` for AsyncLocalStorage in context-storage; future `node:` imports in middleware require explicit ADR-level justification."
    RATIONALE "AsyncLocalStorage is now supported by Node, Bun, Deno, and Cloudflare Workers, making this exception cross-runtime in practice; the exception is not open-ended."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Third-party middleware that requires dependencies (Zod, OpenAPI, Sentry, Prisma) must ship as separate `@hono/*` packages outside this repo."
    RATIONALE "Keeps deps opt-in so they don't affect users who don't install them; preserves the zero-dep guarantee of core."
  INTENT ADR-01 context "Core uses a single `app.fetch(request, env, ctx)` entry point that runs unmodified on any supported runtime."
    RATIONALE "Because core only uses Web Standards, the same fetch signature works everywhere; adapters produce that call."
  INTENT ADR-01 context "Per-adapter helpers may legitimately import runtime primitives (e.g., node:fs/promises in bun adapter, node:path in deno adapter, node:crypto in lambda-edge adapter)."
    RATIONALE "These imports only execute on runtimes that provide those modules."
  INTENT ADR-01 context "Utilities like cookie parsing, query parsing, MIME detection, base64, JWT, UUID, and body reading are hand-implemented in `src/utils/**` rather than delegated to libraries."
    RATIONALE "Deliberate cost of the zero-dep rule; needed to keep hono/tiny under 12 kB and respect the Workers 1 MB compressed bundle limit."
  INTENT docstring:src/helper/proxy/index.ts context "proxy wrapper mirrors fetch's parameters and return value, except for proxy-specific options"
    RATIONALE "Establishes API surface compatibility with fetch"
